interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, mature platform work
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design data quality and verification pipelines

coding: At ZoomInfo, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at ZoomInfo scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, ownership, mature platform work.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design data quality and verification pipelines.
values: ZoomInfo succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does ZoomInfo compare to Apollo or Cognism?

rawSummary:
ZoomInfo Interview Guide (2026): B2B Data Platform Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

ZoomInfo
zoominfo.com ↗

ZoomInfo is the largest B2B data and intelligence platform — public since 2020. Owns Chorus, Insent, RingLead, and others. The interview emphasizes data engineering at extreme scale (billions of contacts), data quality, and the unique challenges of public-record-based intelligence.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 4–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a B2B contact graph at billions-of-edges scale

- Design data quality and verification pipelines

- Design real-time intent signal tracking

- Coding: medium DSA, often with graph or data-pipeline framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, mature platform work

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $135K–$170K total

- Senior SE: $200K–$260K

- Staff: $290K–$380K

- Principal: $400K–$520K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Go or Java (bulk of the codebase)

- Understand large-scale data engineering and graph databases

- Brush up on B2B sales intelligence patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is ZoomInfo remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Vancouver WA (HQ), Waltham MA, Bethesda. Some engineering roles remote within US.

How does ZoomInfo compare to Apollo or Cognism?

ZoomInfo is the enterprise leader by data depth. Apollo competes on SMB. Cognism is European-strong. ZoomInfo pays mid-tier; below FAANG.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, customer-driven, calmer pace post-2022 macro challenges. Strong work-life balance.
